name: Lablebi
description: A warming Tunisian street-food breakfast of chickpeas in cumin-spiced broth, topped with a poached egg, harissa, and stale bread cubes.
steps: Heat chickpeas in their broth with cumin, garlic, and a pinch of salt.
Tear stale bread into small pieces and place them in a deep bowl.
Pour the hot chickpea broth over the bread to soften it.
Poach or fry the egg and place it on top.
Add harissa, a drizzle of olive oil, and tuna if desired. Serve immediately while hot.

name: Fricassé Tunisien (Homemade)
description: Small fried dough rolls filled with tuna, harissa, boiled egg, and olives — the iconic Tunisian breakfast sandwich.
steps: Make a simple yeast dough with flour, water, yeast, oil, and salt. Let it rise 20 minutes.
Shape into small oval rolls and fry in oil until golden on both sides.
Drain on paper and cut open while hot.
Fill with tuna, sliced boiled egg, harissa, and olives.
Serve immediately.

name: Shakshuka (Ojja Tomate)
description: Eggs poached directly in a spiced tomato and pepper sauce — a quick and satisfying Tunisian breakfast.
steps: Dice tomatoes and pepper finely.
Sauté in olive oil with garlic and harissa until sauce thickens.
Make two wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each.
Cover and cook on low heat 4-5 minutes until whites are set.
Serve directly from the pan with bread.

name: Asida with Honey and Olive Oil
description: A smooth, thick semolina porridge — the traditional Tunisian winter breakfast, drizzled with honey and olive oil.
steps: Bring water or milk to a boil with a pinch of salt.
Gradually whisk in the semolina, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Cook on low heat 10-15 minutes until thick and pulling away from the sides.
Transfer to a bowl, make a well in the center, and drizzle with honey and olive oil.
Serve hot.

name: Msemen with Olive Oil and Honey
description: Pan-fried laminated Tunisian flatbread served with extra-virgin olive oil and honey for a simple, satisfying breakfast.
steps: Mix flours, salt, oil, and warm water into a soft dough. Rest 15 minutes.
Divide into balls, flatten with oiled hands into thin squares.
Fold edges inward repeatedly to create layers.
Cook on a dry non-stick pan, 3 minutes each side.
Serve warm drizzled with olive oil and honey.

name: Eggs with Merguez and Tomato
description: Fried eggs cooked alongside spiced merguez sausage and diced tomatoes — a hearty Tunisian weekend breakfast.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
substitutes: No oil needed — merguez releases fat

steps: Slice the merguez and cook in a non-stick pan on medium heat.
Add diced tomato and cook until softened.
Push everything to the edges and crack the eggs into the center.
Cook to preference (runny or set) and serve with bread.

name: Oatmeal with Banana and Dates
description: Rolled oats cooked in milk, topped with sliced banana and two Deglet Nour dates — a slow-release energy breakfast adapted for Tunisian tastes.
steps: Bring milk to a simmer in a small saucepan.
Add oats and stir on medium heat for 5 minutes until creamy.
Transfer to a bowl and top with sliced banana and chopped dates.
Add a pinch of cinnamon if desired. Serve immediately.

name: Greek Yogurt with Honey and Walnuts
description: Thick plain yogurt topped with local honey and a handful of walnuts — a high-protein, no-cook breakfast ready in one minute.
steps: Spoon yogurt into a bowl.
Drizzle honey on top.
Add walnuts and serve immediately.

name: Pain Grillé with Tuna and Harissa
description: Toasted baguette slices spread with harissa and topped with canned tuna, olives, and a drizzle of olive oil — the everyday Tunisian quick breakfast.
steps: Toast the baguette slices.
Spread a thin layer of harissa on each slice.
Top with tuna, olives, and a drizzle of olive oil.
Serve immediately with a glass of mint tea or black coffee.

name: Boiled Eggs with Olive Oil and Za'atar
description: Two hard-boiled eggs served with olive oil for dipping and a pinch of dried thyme — simple, protein-rich, and requiring zero cooking skill.
steps: Place eggs in cold water, bring to boil, cook 10 minutes.
Run under cold water and peel.
Serve halved with a small bowl of olive oil for dipping and a pinch of thyme.

name: Khobz Tabouna with Olive Oil and Cheese
description: Traditional clay-oven Tunisian bread served warm with a drizzle of olive oil and a triangle of La Vache Qui Rit — found in every Tunisian household.
steps: Warm the bread in a pan or oven for 2 minutes.
Drizzle olive oil over the warm bread.
Spread the cheese portions on top.
Serve with mint tea.

name: Hsou (Barley Porridge)
description: A rustic Tunisian winter breakfast porridge made from barley flour, olive oil, and a hint of honey — warming and very high in fibre.
steps: Bring water to a boil.
Whisk in barley flour gradually to avoid lumps.
Stir on low heat 10-12 minutes until thick.
Serve in a bowl with olive oil drizzled in the center and honey on top.

name: Banana Milkshake with Peanut Butter
description: A blended milk, banana, and peanut butter shake — quick post-workout breakfast or busy morning meal.
steps: Blend banana, milk, and peanut butter together.
Pour into a glass and drink immediately.

name: Fried Egg Sandwich (Kesra Beyd)
description: A fried egg in a half-baguette with harissa, olive, and tomato slice — the fast Tunisian breakfast eaten before school or work.
steps: Fry the egg in a tiny bit of olive oil.
Cut the baguette open and spread harissa inside.
Add tomato slices, then place the fried egg inside.
Eat immediately while hot.

name: Fruit Salad with Raïb (Fresh Yogurt)
description: Seasonal diced fruits — orange, apple, banana — served alongside a pot of cold Raïb, the Tunisian fermented milk yogurt.
steps: Dice the apple and banana, segment the orange.
Mix in a bowl.
Serve alongside a glass of cold Raïb.

name: Grilled Chicken Thighs with Roasted Vegetables
description: Marinated chicken thighs grilled until golden, served with roasted zucchini, peppers, and tomatoes.
steps: Marinate chicken with olive oil, garlic, turmeric, salt, and pepper.
Cut vegetables into large chunks, toss with olive oil and salt.
Grill chicken 12-15 minutes each side until cooked through.
Roast vegetables at 200°C for 25 minutes.
Serve together on a plate.

name: Chorba Lsan Asfour (Vermicelli Soup)
description: A comforting light Tunisian soup made with tiny pasta (bird tongue), tomato, celery, and either chicken or beef.
steps: Brown meat with tomato paste, cumin, and garlic.
Add 800ml water and bring to a boil.
Add celery and simmer 20 minutes.
Add orzo and cook a further 8 minutes until tender.
Serve hot with a squeeze of lemon.

name: Baked Fish with Chermoula and Potato
description: A whole fish or fillet baked over sliced potatoes and tomatoes in a herb chermoula marinade.
steps: Slice potatoes and tomatoes, layer in an oven dish.
Mix garlic, parsley, cumin, lemon, and olive oil to make chermoula.
Coat the fish with chermoula and place on the vegetables.
Bake at 190°C for 30-35 minutes until fish is cooked through.
Serve directly from the dish.

name: Harissa Pasta (Macarouna Harissa)
description: Spaghetti cooked in a simple Tunisian harissa-tomato sauce — the weeknight dinner in most Tunisian households.
steps: Boil pasta in salted water until al dente.
Sauté minced garlic in olive oil, add harissa and tomato paste.
Add a ladle of pasta water to the sauce and simmer 5 minutes.
Drain pasta and toss in the sauce.
Serve with grated cheese if available.

name: Kafteji (Traditional Fried Version) — Smaller Portion
description: The classic Tunisian fried vegetable and egg dish — a smaller, controlled portion for dinner.
steps: Fry potato, pepper, and tomato separately until softened.
Drain excess oil on paper towels.
Chop vegetables together with two knives on a board.
Add beaten eggs to the chopped vegetables and cook until set.
Serve warm with a side of Mechouia or salad.

name: Stuffed Peppers with Rice and Minced Beef
description: Bell peppers stuffed with spiced minced beef and rice, baked in a light tomato sauce.
steps: Mix raw minced beef with parboiled rice, sautéed onion, garlic, and spices.
Cut the tops off peppers and remove seeds.
Fill peppers with the beef-rice mixture.
Place in a baking dish with diluted tomato paste and 200ml water.
Bake covered at 180°C for 40 minutes.

name: Sardines Grillées with Harissa and Salad
description: Whole fresh sardines seasoned with chermoula and grilled on a pan or barbecue, served with a Tunisian salad.
steps: Mix harissa, garlic, lemon juice, and parsley. Coat the sardines.
Grill on high heat 3-4 minutes per side.
While grilling, prepare the salad.
Serve immediately with salad and bread if desired.

name: Lentil and Vegetable Soup
description: A thick, filling red lentil soup with carrots, potatoes, and cumin — a complete dinner in a bowl.
steps: Sauté garlic in olive oil, add cumin.
Add rinsed lentils, diced carrot and potato, and 700ml water.
Boil then simmer 25 minutes until lentils dissolve.
Blend partially for a thick texture.
Finish with lemon juice and serve hot.

name: Turkey Escalope with Ratatouille Tunisienne
description: Pan-fried turkey escalope served alongside a warm slow-cooked medley of summer vegetables in olive oil.
steps: Dice all vegetables and cook slowly in olive oil with garlic and thyme.
Season the turkey escalope with salt, pepper, and lemon.
Pan-fry escalope 3-4 minutes per side until golden.
Serve the escalope on a bed of warm ratatouille.

name: Rechta (Tunisian Flat Pasta with Chicken Broth)
description: Homemade flat pasta strips cooked in a light chicken and onion broth, scented with cinnamon and rosewater.
steps: Simmer chicken and onion in water with spices 30 minutes to make broth.
Add chickpeas and cook 10 minutes more.
Cook pasta separately in salted water.
Arrange pasta in a deep dish, pour the broth with chicken and chickpeas on top.
Garnish with cinnamon and a few drops of rosewater.

name: Steamed Chicken with Couscous and Harissa Broth
description: A lighter single-serving version of the Friday couscous — steamed grain with one piece of chicken and a small cup of broth.
steps: Brown chicken with tomato paste, onion, and harissa.
Add 500ml water and vegetables. Simmer 30 minutes.
Pour couscous in a bowl, add 1:1 boiling broth, cover 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
Serve couscous topped with chicken and vegetables.
Serve a small cup of broth on the side.

name: Kefta Meatballs in Tomato Sauce
description: Small spiced beef or chicken meatballs simmered in a simple harissa-tomato sauce, served with bread or on their own.
steps: Mix minced meat with grated onion, parsley, cumin, salt, and pepper. Form small balls.
Brown meatballs in a lightly oiled pan on all sides.
Blend tomatoes and add to the pan with harissa.
Simmer together 20 minutes until sauce thickens.
Serve with bread or over steamed rice.

name: Spinach and Cheese Omelette
description: A filling two-egg omelette loaded with fresh spinach and a slice of melted cheese — quick weeknight dinner.
steps: Wilt the spinach in a dry pan 2 minutes. Set aside.
Beat eggs with salt and pepper.
Cook in a non-stick pan until nearly set.
Add spinach and cheese to one half, fold over.
Serve immediately.

name: Tuna and Chickpea Salad Dinner
description: A no-cook high-protein cold dinner salad of chickpeas, tuna, tomato, and parsley with an olive oil dressing.
steps: Drain and rinse chickpeas.
Dice tomato and roughly chop parsley.
Combine everything in a bowl.
Dress with olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Serve cold.

name: Tajine Tunisien (Egg and Cheese Bake)
description: Not the Moroccan version — the Tunisian Tajine is a baked egg, cheese, and meat frittata, served in slices.
steps: Cook and finely crumble the meat with onion and spices.
Beat eggs with mashed potato, cheese, cooked meat, and parsley.
Pour into an oiled oven dish.
Bake at 180°C for 25 minutes until firm and golden on top.
Cut into squares and serve warm.

name: Dates with Almonds
description: Three Deglet Nour dates each stuffed with an almond — the most accessible and culturally natural Tunisian snack.
steps: Remove the pit from each date.
Insert an almond inside each date.
Eat immediately.

name: Plain Yogurt with a Drizzle of Honey
description: A small pot of plain Tunisian yogurt with a teaspoon of honey — low-calorie, high-protein afternoon snack.
steps: Spoon yogurt into a bowl.
Drizzle honey on top.
Eat immediately.

name: Apple with Peanut Butter
description: A medium apple sliced and served with a tablespoon of peanut butter for dipping — satisfying and blood-sugar stable.
steps: Slice the apple.
Dip in peanut butter and eat.

name: Hard-Boiled Egg with Za'atar
description: One hard-boiled egg with a pinch of dried thyme and a few drops of olive oil — the simplest high-protein snack.
steps: Boil egg for 10 minutes, run under cold water, peel.
Sprinkle with thyme and a few drops of olive oil.

name: Handful of Mixed Nuts
description: A small mixed portion of almonds, walnuts, and peanuts — available in every Tunisian grocery and souk.
steps: Measure out the mixed nuts.
Eat as is.

name: Makroudh (1 piece)
description: A single traditional Tunisian semolina pastry filled with date paste and fried or baked — one piece as a controlled sweet snack.
steps: Eat as a standalone sweet snack.
Pair with unsweetened mint tea.

name: Banana with a Glass of Milk
description: A ripe banana eaten with a glass of cold milk — the simplest post-training snack for muscle recovery.
steps: Peel banana.
Eat with a glass of cold milk.

name: Cucumber and Carrot Sticks with Hummus
description: Raw cucumber and carrot sticks served with a small portion of hummus for dipping — crunchy, filling, and very low in calories.
steps: Cut cucumber and carrot into sticks.
Serve alongside hummus for dipping.

name: Orange and a Square of Dark Chocolate
description: A whole peeled orange with one small square of dark chocolate (70%) — satisfies sweet cravings with minimal calories.
steps: Peel the orange.
Eat with the chocolate square.

name: Peanut Butter on Whole Wheat Toast
description: One slice of whole wheat toast spread with peanut butter — a filling afternoon snack that keeps hunger away until dinner.
steps: Toast the bread.
Spread peanut butter and eat.

name: Watermelon Slice (Summer)
description: A generous slice of cold watermelon — the quintessential Tunisian summer snack, zero prep, extremely hydrating.
steps: Cut a slice and eat cold.

name: Lait Ribot with a Date
description: A glass of cold Lait Ribot (Tunisian fermented buttermilk) with one date — a probiotic-rich snack found in every Tunisian fridge.
steps: Pour cold Lait Ribot into a glass.
Eat the date alongside.

name: Salade Tunisienne with Grilled Chicken
description: A classic finely chopped Tunisian cucumber and tomato salad served with grilled chicken breast.
steps: Finely chop the tomatoes, cucumber, and onion.
Mix with olive oil, salt, pepper, and a squeeze of lemon.
Season the chicken and grill 5-7 minutes each side.
Serve the grilled chicken alongside the salad.

name: Ojja with Tuna
description: A quick and spicy Tunisian tomato and egg stew topped with canned tuna.
steps: Sauté minced garlic and harissa in olive oil.
Add diced tomatoes and simmer until thick.
Stir in the tuna, crack eggs directly into the sauce.
Cover and cook low heat 3-5 minutes.
Serve hot, optionally with bread.

name: Loubia with Beef
description: A hearty high-fiber tomato-based stew with white beans and lean beef.
steps: Sauté beef with olive oil, garlic, and tomato paste.
Add water and spices, bring to a boil.
Add soaked white beans, simmer 45-60 min until tender.
Serve warm with bread.

name: Chorba Frik with Chicken
description: A traditional Tunisian crushed green wheat soup made with chicken and celery.
steps: Sauté chicken with tomato paste, celery, and spices.
Add water and chickpeas, bring to boil.
Add rinsed Frik and simmer 30 minutes.
Serve hot with lemon.

name: Couscous Bel Khodra with Chicken
description: A balanced portion of Tunisian couscous with carrots, zucchini, pumpkin, and lean chicken.
steps: Prepare red sauce by simmering chicken, tomato paste, onions, chickpeas.
Steam couscous over boiling sauce in couscoussier.
Add vegetables halfway through cooking.
Mix couscous with sauce, top with chicken and vegetables.

name: Market 3das (Tunisian Lentil Stew)
description: A thick, nutritious lentil stew with carrots, garlic, and cumin.
steps: Sauté garlic in olive oil.
Add lentils, diced carrot, cumin, and water.
Boil then simmer 25-30 minutes until thick.

name: Grilled Daurade with Tastira
description: Grilled sea bream served with a side of chopped peppers, tomatoes, and egg.
steps: Season fish with salt, cumin, lemon. Grill until cooked.
Grill peppers and tomatoes, chop finely.
Fry egg and mix into chopped vegetables (Tastira).
Serve fish with Tastira.

name: Salade Mechouia with Grilled Turkey
description: Grilled and mashed peppers and tomatoes served with lean turkey.
steps: Grill peppers, tomatoes, and garlic until charred.
Peel and finely chop. Season with olive oil, salt, caraway.
Grill turkey until fully cooked.
Serve together.

name: Plat Tunisien (Healthy Version)
description: A deconstructed sandwich plate: tuna, boiled eggs, small potato, harissa.
steps: Boil eggs and potato.
Arrange potato, eggs, tuna on a plate.
Add harissa and salad on the side.

name: Tunisian Macarouna with Chicken
description: Spaghetti in a rich garlicky Tunisian tomato sauce with chicken.
steps: Boil pasta until al dente.
Sauté chicken, garlic, tomato paste. Add water and spices.
Simmer until sauce thickens.
Toss pasta in sauce.

name: Healthy Oven-Baked Kafteji
description: A lighter baked version of traditional Kafteji with eggs.
steps: Cube vegetables, toss with oil and tabil, bake 200°C 25 min.
Fry eggs separately.
Chop baked vegetables and eggs together.
Serve warm.

name: Kamounia (Beef and Liver Stew)
description: A rich cumin-scented stew with beef chunks and liver.
steps: Sauté onion, beef, tomato paste in oil.
Add water, simmer until beef nearly tender.
Add liver and generous cumin.
Cook 10 more minutes until sauce is thick.

name: Jelbana with Chicken (Pea Stew)
description: A savory stew of green peas, artichoke hearts, and chicken.
steps: Sauté onion and chicken with tomato paste.
Add water, season with turmeric and pepper.
Add peas and artichoke, simmer 30 minutes.

name: Brik au Four with Salad
description: Baked brik filled with tuna, egg, and parsley, served with green salad.
steps: Mix parsley, tuna, salt.
Place on Malsouka, crack egg inside, fold into triangle.
Brush with oil, bake 200°C 10-15 min.
Serve with salad.

name: Western-style Tuna Pasta Salad
description: Cold pasta salad with tuna, corn, and olive oil vinaigrette.
steps: Boil and cool pasta.
Mix all ingredients.
Dress and serve cold.

name: Rouz Jerbi (Djerbian Rice)
description: Traditional steamed rice with spinach, parsley, and lean chicken.
steps: Mix rice with spinach, parsley, diced chicken, tomato paste, and spices.
Steam in couscoussier 40-45 minutes.

name: Omelette Tastira
description: Eggs beaten into sautéed tomatoes and peppers.
steps: Sauté diced tomato and pepper until soft.
Beat eggs with tabil, pour over vegetables.
Cook until set. Fold and serve.

name: Chicken and Potato Traybake (Koucha Djaj)
description: Chicken and potato wedges in Tunisian spices, roasted in the oven.
steps: Toss chicken, potato wedges, onion with olive oil and spices.
Spread on tray, bake 200°C 35-40 minutes.

name: Sautéed Green Beans with Minced Beef
description: A low-carb western-style lunch of green beans and garlic minced beef.
steps: Steam green beans until tender-crisp.
Brown minced beef with garlic.
Toss green beans with beef.
Season and serve hot.

name: Slata Besbes with Grilled Escalope
description: Raw fennel and orange salad with grilled turkey escalope.
steps: Thinly slice fennel, cube orange, toss with olive oil, lemon, olives.
Grill turkey until golden.
Serve salad alongside turkey.
